title: Duration of untreated psychosis as predictor of long-term outcome in schizophrenia: systematic review and meta-analysis.
finding_one_line: Across 33 samples, longer DUP shows small but consistent correlations (0.13-0.18) with poorer long-term symptomatic, functional and remission outcomes.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: pooled correlations between long DUP and poor outcomes
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Long DUP correlated statistically significantly with poor general symptomatic outcome, more severe positive and negative symptoms, lesser likelihood of remission and poor social functioning and global outcome (correlations 0.13-0.18).

claims_it_can_support: DUP is a modifiable predictor of outcome
Longer DUP associated with worse outcomes (small, consistent)

title: Comparative efficacy and tolerability of 32 oral antipsychotics for the acute treatment of adults with multi-episode schizophrenia: a systematic review and network meta-analysis.
finding_one_line: Network meta-analysis of 402 trials (53,463 participants), 32 antipsychotics: all beat placebo (SMD -0.89 clozapine to -0.03 levomepromazine); efficacy differences gradual, side-effect differences marked.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: range of overall symptom reduction across 32 antipsychotics
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: standardised mean differences ranging from -0.89 (95% CrI -1.08 to -0.71) for clozapine to -0.03 (-0.59 to 0.52) for levomepromazine (40 815 participants).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: range of mean weight gain vs placebo (metabolic burden)
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Mean differences compared to placebo for weight gain (28 317 participants) ranged from -0.16 kg (-0.73 to 0.40; ziprasidone) to 3.21 kg (2.10 to 4.31; zotepine).

claims_it_can_support: All antipsychotics more effective than placebo
Efficacy differences gradual; side-effect differences marked
Agents vary in weight gain, prolactin, QTc

title: Comparative efficacy and tolerability of 15 antipsychotic drugs in schizophrenia: a multiple-treatments meta-analysis.
finding_one_line: Multiple-treatments meta-analysis of 212 trials (43,049): all 15 antipsychotics beat placebo (SMD 0.88 clozapine to 0.33 iloperidone); substantial side-effect differences (EPS OR 0.30-4.76).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: efficacy vs placebo across 15 antipsychotics
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The standardised mean differences with 95% credible intervals were: clozapine 0.88, 0.73-1.03; ... lurasidone 0.33, 0.21-0.45; and iloperidone 0.33, 0.22-0.43.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: range of odds ratios vs placebo for extrapyramidal side-effects
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: for extrapyramidal side-effects 0.30 (clozapine) to 4.76 (haloperidol).

claims_it_can_support: All 15 antipsychotics significantly more effective than placebo
Efficacy differences small but robust; side-effect differences substantial

title: Antipsychotic drugs versus placebo for relapse prevention in schizophrenia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
finding_one_line: 65 trials (6493 patients): maintenance antipsychotics cut 1-year relapse from 64% (placebo) to 27% (RR 0.40, NNT 3) and readmission from 26% to 10%, at cost of more weight gain, movement disorders, sedation.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: 1-year relapse on antipsychotic vs placebo, RR and NNT
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Antipsychotic drugs significantly reduced relapse rates at 1 year (drugs 27%vs placebo 64%; risk ratio [RR] 0.40, 95% CI 0.33-0.49; number needed to treat to benefit [NNTB] 3, 95% CI 2-3).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: hospital readmission on antipsychotic vs placebo
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Fewer patients given antipsychotic drugs than placebo were readmitted (10%vs 26%; RR 0.38, 95% CI 0.27-0.55; NNTB 5, 4-9).

claims_it_can_support: Maintenance more than halves 1-year relapse
~3 patients treated to prevent one relapse
Benefit weighed against weight gain, movement disorders, sedation

title: Cognitive-behavioural therapy for the symptoms of schizophrenia: systematic review and meta-analysis with examination of potential bias.
finding_one_line: CBT for schizophrenia symptoms has a small pooled effect (overall -0.33) that shrinks to -0.15 in blinded/masked trials.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: pooled effect size CBT vs comparator, overall symptoms
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Pooled effect sizes were -0.33 (95% CI -0.47 to -0.19) in 34 studies of overall symptoms, -0.25 (95% CI -0.37 to -0.13) in 33 studies of positive symptoms and -0.13 (95% CI -0.25 to -0.01) in 34 studies of negative symptoms.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: effect for overall symptoms when unblinded vs blinded — bias inflates estimate
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Masking significantly moderated effect size in the meta-analyses of overall symptoms (effect sizes -0.62 (95% CI -0.88 to -0.35) v. -0.15 (95% CI -0.27 to -0.03), P = 0.001).

claims_it_can_support: CBT effect on symptoms is 'small'
Apparent effect shrinks when bias controlled
CBT is adjunct, not replacement

title: Family intervention for schizophrenia.
finding_one_line: Cochrane review of 53 RCTs: family intervention reduces relapse (RR 0.55, NNT 7), hospital admission (RR 0.78, NNT 8), improves medication adherence (RR 0.60, NNT 6).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: relapse reduction with family intervention vs standard care
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Family intervention may decrease the frequency of relapse (n = 2981, 32 RCTs, RR 0.55 CI 0.5 to 0.6, NNT 7 CI 6 to 8).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: improved adherence with family intervention
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: encourage compliance with medication (n = 695, 10 RCTs, RR 0.60 CI 0.5 to 0.7, NNT 6 CI 5 to 9).

claims_it_can_support: Family intervention reduces relapse and hospitalisation
High expressed emotion predicts relapse
A large, under-recognised relapse-prevention lever

title: Comprehensive Versus Usual Community Care for First-Episode Psychosis: 2-Year Outcomes From the NIMH RAISE Early Treatment Program.
finding_one_line: RAISE-ETP RCT (34 clinics, N=404): coordinated specialty care (NAVIGATE) improved quality of life, psychopathology and work/school vs usual care over 2 years, larger gains when DUP shorter (<74-week median).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: coordinated specialty care improved outcomes vs usual community care
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The 223 recipients of NAVIGATE remained in treatment longer, experienced greater improvement in quality of life and psychopathology, and experienced greater involvement in work and school compared with 181 participants in community care.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: median duration of untreated psychosis; moderator threshold
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The median duration of untreated psychosis was 74 weeks.

claims_it_can_support: Coordinated specialty care improves outcomes vs usual care
Benefit larger when DUP shorter

title: Comparison of Early Intervention Services vs Treatment as Usual for Early-Phase Psychosis: A Systematic Review, Meta-analysis, and Meta-regression.
finding_one_line: Meta-analysis of 10 RCTs (2176 patients): early intervention services beat treatment-as-usual on all 13 outcomes — fewer discontinuations (RR 0.70), fewer hospitalisations (RR 0.74), more school/work (RR 1.13), lower symptoms (SMD -0.32).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: overall superiority of EIS vs TAU
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Across 10 randomized clinical trials ... among 2176 patients ... EIS was associated with better outcomes than TAU at the end of treatment for all 13 meta-analyzable outcomes.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: key effect sizes EIS vs TAU
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: at least 1 psychiatric hospitalization (RR, 0.74; 95% CI, 0.61-0.90; P = .003) ... total symptom severity (standardized mean difference [SMD], -0.32; 95% CI, -0.47 to -0.17; P < .001).

claims_it_can_support: Early intervention superior to usual care across every measured outcome

title: Next-step treatment for schizophrenia non-responsive to antipsychotics: a systematic review and network meta-analysis.
finding_one_line: Network meta-analysis of 59 RCTs on antipsychotic non-response: ECT augmentation (SMD -0.51) and combination (SMD -0.25) show very-weak, cautiously-considered benefit — ECT a narrow next-step, not first-line.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: effect of ECT added to antipsychotic vs continuing same in non-responders
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: electroconvulsive therapy (ECT; k = 5; n = 97; SMD -0.51, -0.96 to -0.06; very low) might be more efficacious than continuing the same antipsychotic.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: certainty framing for ECT and combination augmentation
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Very weak evidence suggested efficacy of antipsychotic combination and ECT augmentation and they might be considered, but with substantial caution.

claims_it_can_support: ECT has a narrow, cautious role in antipsychotic-non-responsive cases, not routine

title: What is Minimally Adequate Treatment of Psychosis and Should Duration of Inadequate Treatment be a Clinical and Research Target? A Perspective and State-of-the-Art Review.
finding_one_line: State-of-the-art review: antipsychotic non-adherence is common (44-56%) and repeatedly linked to worse outcomes (relapse RR up to 2.70, mortality RR up to 1.83) — reframing inadequate treatment as widespread, not a moral failing.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: rates of antipsychotic non-adherence
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Inadequate antipsychotic treatment is common (non-adherence rates = 44%-56%), and repeatedly associated with poorer outcomes across several key patient-centered outcomes, including increased risk of relapse (relative risk (RR) up to 2.70...) and higher mortality (RR up to 1.83, n = 272 030).

claims_it_can_support: Non-adherence affects ~half of patients; a common, not exceptional, occurrence
Inadequate treatment associated with higher relapse and mortality

title: Tardive Dyskinesia Prevalence in the Period of Second-Generation Antipsychotic Use: A Meta-Analysis.
finding_one_line: Meta-analysis of 41 studies (11,493 patients): global tardive dyskinesia prevalence 25.3%, lower with second-generation (20.7%) than first-generation (30.0%).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: global mean tardive dyskinesia prevalence
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The global mean TD prevalence was 25.3% (95% CI = 22.7%-28.1%) across all 41 studies (N = 11,493...).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: TD prevalence during second-generation vs first-generation treatment
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Rates were lower with current SGA treatment (20.7%...) vs current FGA treatment (30.0%...).

claims_it_can_support: TD affects roughly a quarter of treated patients
Less frequent with second-generation but not eliminated

title: Do antipsychotics work in people with schizophrenia? A review of outcomes and effect sizes.
finding_one_line: Narrative review framing effect sizes: antipsychotics improve symptoms with a small-medium effect (~0.4 vs placebo) rising to ~0.9 for relapse prevention (NNT ~3). Framing/corroboration only.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: typical acute-treatment effect size (framing, corroborates Huhn/Leucht)
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: positive psychotic symptoms and global psychopathology improve with a small-medium effect size of about 0.4 versus placebo.

claims_it_can_support: Antipsychotics have a small-medium effect comparable to many general-medicine drugs (framing only)
notes: 2025 pub; T3 framing — not sole support for a primary number.
